literal_meaning: Not-this / world / there-is / of-one-who-does-not-sacrifice / how / other / O-best-of-Kurus
contextual_meaning: A life devoid of contribution, ritual, or intentional offering creates inner drought; without this psychic nourishment even present comforts feel hollow, let alone any hope of future flourishing.
psychological_state: Eriksonian stagnation: the unconscious dread that one is taking more from life than giving, producing low-grade despair masked by consumerism or busyness.
trigger_scenario: Mid-career burnout where achievements feel meaningless because they serve only the self; Sunday evening emptiness despite a full calendar.
anchor_text: Give something of yourself each day; only offering keeps life from drying up.
